signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.73 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite Middle East tensions. Saudi Arabia and Kuwait lifting US military base restrictions and Hormuz corridor uncertainty introduce intervention risk on any test of 160.00. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains crowded; carry dominates while VIX at 17.4 indicates cross-asset stress is contained. Watch for BoJ verbal warnings if price tests 160.00.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Divergence
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.151% as NZD outperforms (+0.614%) while CAD lags (-0.537%). NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias, creating short-squeeze risk ahead of the 27 May meeting. CAD underperformance is driven by vs USD and neutral BoC bias. NZD/USD at 0.5954, USD/CAD at 1.3639.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 89% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. Retail is fading the institutional long as AUD/USD tests 0.7230. RBA at 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry premium supporting the AUD bid. Retail capitulation risk rises if price extends higher. Watch for LF delta turning negative to shift the bullish read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.727 vs norm of -0.130 (z=+1.83σ) — EUR/USD decoupling from equity weakness as ECB's Schnabel flags irreversible damage from Iran conflict. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) structurally limits EUR/USD upside at 1.1751. Market is pricing a hold for the 11 Jun ECB meeting. Watch for 1.1800 resistance to confirm if the correlation break persists.
